meta.title: The Cow and the Tiger — Set 5
meta.subject: English Reading
meta.description: Challenge quiz for 'The Cow and the Tiger' — inference, moral, and advanced skills.

questions.question: What does the moral 'Always keep your promises' mean?
options: Only keep your promises if it is easy
Do what you said you would do, no matter what
Promises are not important
Always make many promises every day
questions.explanation: 'Always keep your promises' means always do what you said you would do. Bala showed this — even though it was scary, she went back to the tiger.

questions.question: The moral of the story says: 'Always tell the truth.'
questions.explanation: Yes! The moral at the end is: 'Always keep your promises. Always tell the truth.' Honesty is the main lesson of the story.

questions.question: Why did Bala ask the tiger to let her go?
options: She wanted to find more grass in the forest
She needed to go feed her baby calf
She was afraid and wanted to escape forever
She wanted to call for help
questions.explanation: Bala said 'Please don't eat me! Let me go feed my calf.' She needed to go home to feed her baby calf — that was her reason.

questions.question: Bala kept her _______. She came back to the tiger just as she said she would.
questions.hint: 'Kept her ___' means she did what she promised
questions.explanation: 'Keeping your word' means doing what you promised. Bala 'kept her word' by returning to the tiger.

questions.question: Why was the tiger 'surprised' when Bala came back?
options: He forgot about Bala
He thought Bala would run away and not return
He was surprised because Bala came back with food
He was surprised because Bala brought other animals
questions.explanation: The tiger expected Bala to escape. He was surprised because most animals would run and never return. Bala's honesty was unexpected and impressed him.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form the story's moral:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'Always tell the truth.' This is the second part of the story's moral — honesty is always important.

questions.question: Sort — did each event make Bala HAPPY or SAD?
items: The tiger promised to protect her
The tiger said he would attack

categories.label: Made Bala Happy
answers: The tiger promised to protect her

categories.label: Made Bala Sad
answers: The tiger said he would attack

questions.explanation: Bala was sad when she was caught and threatened. She was happy when she fed her calf and when the tiger became her protector.

questions.question: Bala going back to the forest was easy and not scary at all.
questions.explanation: Returning to the tiger was very scary — the tiger had threatened to eat her! But Bala returned because she made a promise. That is what makes her brave and honest.

questions.question: How did Bala's honesty change the tiger?
options: The tiger became angry and ate her
The tiger ignored Bala and left
The tiger was so impressed that he forgave her and became her protector
The tiger learned to graze in the forest
questions.explanation: Bala's honesty deeply impressed the tiger. He said 'You told the truth. Now I won't attack.' He even promised to protect her. Honesty changed his heart completely.

questions.question: Bala was an _______ cow — she always did what she said she would do.
questions.hint: She never lied; she always kept her word
questions.explanation: Bala was 'honest' — she always did what she said. 'Brave' also works because returning to the tiger took great courage.

questions.question: Which sentence BEST describes the theme of 'The Cow and the Tiger'?
options: Animals in the forest are dangerous and scary
Honesty and keeping promises can change people's hearts
Cows are smarter than tigers
Never go into a shadowy forest alone
questions.explanation: The theme is that honesty and keeping promises can change hearts. Bala's honesty turned a dangerous tiger into a gentle protector.

questions.question: Arrange the words to describe what the tiger did at the end:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'Tiger promised to protect Bala.' This shows how much the tiger changed — from predator to protector.

questions.question: 'The Cow and the Tiger' is a fable because it has animal characters and teaches a moral lesson.
questions.explanation: Yes! A fable is a short story with animal characters that teaches a moral lesson. 'The Cow and the Tiger' fits — it has a cow and a tiger, and ends with a clear moral: 'Always keep your promises. Always tell the truth.'

questions.question: Sort these words from the story — describe Bala or describe Tiger at the START of the story.
questions.explanation: Bala was sad (when caught), beautiful (she had a beautiful calf), and brave (she went back). The tiger was angry and caught Bala at the beginning.

questions.question: What does the story teach us about how to deal with difficult situations?
options: Run away as fast as you can
Be dishonest to get what you want
Be honest and brave, even when it is hard
Ask for help from others
questions.explanation: The story teaches us to be honest and brave, even when it is hard. Bala was scared but kept her promise anyway. Her honesty solved everything.
